items.title: Real Postgres for an ORM round-trip
items.scenario: Your repository class uses SQLAlchemy. Unit tests mock the session — but the schema migration changed the `tags` column to JSONB.
items.explanation: Spin up a real Postgres via testcontainers. Run migrations. Insert, query, update, delete. Catches the JSONB-vs-text drift the moment migrations land — unit tests would still pass.

items.title: VCR cassette for an external LLM provider
items.scenario: Your code calls Cohere's rerank API; you want repeatable tests without burning tokens on every run.
items.explanation: Use `vcrpy` / `pytest-recording`. First run records the real HTTP exchange to a YAML cassette. Subsequent runs replay it. Re-record on demand (e.g. monthly or when the API changes).

items.title: End-to-end RAG query against an in-memory FAISS
items.scenario: Embed → write → query → top-k → format. You want to verify the whole flow works, but don't want a real Pinecone account in CI.
items.explanation: Use an in-memory FAISS index. Real embeddings (or a tiny deterministic fake model). Real query path. Asserts that retrieval returns expected documents — wiring verified.

items.title: Contract test against the upstream OpenAPI
items.scenario: Your service consumes the company's payments API. They publish an OpenAPI spec.
items.explanation: Generate a mock server from the spec; run integration tests against it. When upstream changes the spec, your tests fail before you deploy a broken consumer.

items.title: Smoke test against the real LLM (sparingly)
items.scenario: You want CI to alert when OpenAI deprecates `gpt-4-turbo`.
items.explanation: One test in a separate `@pytest.mark.smoke` suite. Runs daily, not per-commit. Asserts a known prompt returns a non-empty response and the right schema. Fail loud when the contract changes.
